text: You have power over your mind - not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
context: Core Stoic principle: focus on what is within your control to achieve inner strength and freedom from external turmoil.

text: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
context: Emphasizes personal responsibility for one's mental state and perspective.

text: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
context: A call to action and integrity rather than endless debate or theoretical virtue.

text: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
context: Famous 'obstacle is the way' teaching: turn adversity into opportunity through perception and will.

text: Everything we hear is an opinion, not a fact. Everything we see is a perspective, not the truth.
context: Encourages critical thinking and detachment from unexamined assumptions.

text: Choose not to be harmed — and you won’t feel harmed. Don’t feel harmed — and you haven’t been.
context: Stoic control over judgment: external events only harm us if we allow them to through our interpretation.

text: It is not death that a man should fear, but he should fear never beginning to live.
context: Urges living fully and purposefully rather than being paralyzed by fear of the inevitable.

text: Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together, but do so with all your heart.
context: Amor fati — loving one's fate and embracing relationships and circumstances as they are.

text: The best revenge is to be unlike him who performed the injury.
context: True strength lies in moral superiority and refusing to let others dictate your character.

text: Do not act as if you had ten thousand years to live. Death hangs over you. While you live, while it is in your power, be good.
context: Memento mori: the urgency of living virtuously now, without delay or excuse.
